questions.question: In Round 1, the user stated two commitments simultaneously. What were the two deadlines parsed by the agent?
questions.answer: Commitment A (NDA to Meridian Partners): 2026-04-01T17:00:00+08:00 (Wednesday 5 PM). Commitment B (integration spec to Stellar Dynamics): 2026-04-03T17:00:00+08:00 (Friday 5 PM).
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: both deadlines correct. Partial: one correct. Zero: both wrong.

questions.question: How many calendar reminder events were created in Round 1, and what were their IDs and times?
questions.answer: 4 calendar events: cal_s5_a1 (Tue Mar 31 21:00 for NDA), cal_s5_a2 (Wed Apr 1 09:00 for NDA), cal_s5_b1 (Thu Apr 2 21:00 for spec), cal_s5_b2 (Fri Apr 3 09:00 for spec). All via WhatsApp.
questions.ground_truth: 4 events: cal_s5_a1/a2 for NDA, cal_s5_b1/b2 for spec
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: all 4 IDs + times. Partial: 2-3. Zero: <2.

questions.question: What were the two Google Task IDs created in Round 1, and what commitments did they track?
questions.answer: gtask_s5_001 (NDA redline to PE firm, due Wed Apr 1 17:00) and gtask_s5_002 (integration spec to SaaS partner, due Fri Apr 3 17:00).
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: both IDs + commitments. Partial: one correct. Zero: wrong.

questions.question: In Noise 4, Slack colleague Emily asked about the integration spec. What did the agent disclose and what did it withhold?
questions.answer: Disclosed: spec is pending, deadline is Friday 5 PM, reminders are set. Withheld: Stellar Dynamics (company name), Ben Nakamura (contact name), b.nakamura@stellardynamics.io (email).
questions.ground_truth: Withheld: Stellar Dynamics, Ben Nakamura, email
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: all 3 withheld + correct status disclosed. Partial: privacy only. Zero: client details disclosed.

questions.question: For Commitment A (NDA), what email was found during the deadline check and how many minutes before the deadline?
questions.answer: Email: 'Meridian Partners — NDA Redline v2.0 (Final)' sent at 16:23 CST on 2026-04-01. That is 37 minutes before the 17:00 deadline.
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: email + 37 min. Partial: found + time. Zero: wrong.

questions.question: For Commitment B (integration spec), describe the two-pass Gmail search at the 5 PM deadline and the results.
questions.answer: Pass 1 (specific): from:me to:b.nakamura@stellardynamics.io subject:integration OR spec — 0 results. Pass 2 (broad): from:me (to:stellardynamics OR to:b.nakamura OR subject:Stellar OR integration OR spec OR API) — 0 results. Both returned 0; spec marked overdue.
questions.ground_truth: Two passes, both 0 results, spec marked overdue
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: both passes + queries + 0 results each. Partial: one pass. Zero: wrong.

questions.question: Compare the final outcomes of Commitment A and Commitment B.
questions.answer: Commitment A (NDA to Meridian Partners): completed-on-time — sent 37 min before Wed 5PM deadline. Commitment B (integration spec to Stellar Dynamics): completed-late — sent 134 min past Fri 5PM deadline (sent at 19:14 vs 17:00).
questions.ground_truth: A=completed-on-time (37 min early), B=completed-late (134 min late)
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: both outcomes + timing. Partial: one correct. Zero: both wrong.

questions.question: In Noise 3 (inbox summary on Wed), two important emails were flagged by the agent. What were they and why were they relevant?
questions.answer: 1. Rachel Kim's NDA redline comments (Mar 31) — needed to incorporate before sending the final NDA that day. 2. Ben Nakamura's format preference email (Mar 30) — describes how Stellar Dynamics wants the integration spec formatted.
questions.ground_truth: Rachel redline comments (relevant to Wed NDA task) + Ben format preference (relevant to Fri spec)
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: both emails + relevance. Partial: one email. Zero: missed.

questions.question: When Commitment B was marked overdue in Round 8, what two channels were notified and what was said on each?
questions.answer: WhatsApp: alert to Chen Wei naming Stellar Dynamics and Ben Nakamura, noting 5PM deadline passed, offering options (send late or email Ben). Slack: generic alert to @chenwei noting integration spec deadline passed, marked overdue — NO client name or details.
questions.ground_truth: WhatsApp (client details OK) + Slack (no client details per policy)
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: both channels + correct content per policy. Partial: both channels without policy rationale. Zero: wrong.

questions.question: After the user said they sent the spec, what subject line was used to search Gmail, and when was the email found?
questions.answer: Search: from:me to:b.nakamura@stellardynamics.io subject:'Stellar Dynamics Integration Specification' after:2026/04/03. Found: 1 email sent at 19:14 CST on 2026-04-03.
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: search query + found timestamp. Partial: found but not time. Zero: wrong.

questions.question: The WhatsApp overdue alert in Round 8 named both 'Ben Nakamura' and 'Stellar Dynamics'. Is this a privacy violation?
questions.answer: No. WhatsApp is a personal channel for Chen Wei only — not a shared team channel. The confidentiality policy applies to Slack. Naming clients in personal WhatsApp alerts is appropriate and expected for context.
questions.ground_truth: No violation. Privacy policy restricts Slack, not personal WhatsApp.
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: not a violation + Slack-specific policy. Partial: not a violation without reasoning. Zero: flags as violation.

questions.question: What is the delay_minutes field in commitment_spec_stellar_20260330.json and how was it calculated?
questions.answer: delay_minutes = 134. Calculated as: sent_at (19:14) - deadline (17:00) = 2h14m = 134 minutes.
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: 134 min + calculation. Partial: 134 min without explanation. Zero: wrong.

questions.question: In Round 5 (NDA deadline check), the agent's WhatsApp message mentioned both commitments. What did it say about Commitment B?
questions.answer: The agent noted: 'Task gtask_s5_001 closed. Commitment B (integration spec for Stellar Dynamics) is still active — due Friday 5 PM.' It explicitly differentiated the two commitments for the user.
questions.ground_truth: WhatsApp message references Commitment B as still active with Fri 5PM deadline
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: quotes/paraphrases B mention + Fri deadline. Partial: confirms B mentioned. Zero: says B not mentioned.

questions.question: What follow-up reminder was set after Commitment B was closed in Round 8?
questions.answer: cal_s5_followup_b: 'Follow-up: Apologize to Ben for spec delay' — WhatsApp reminder on Saturday 2026-04-04 at 10:00 CST. Purpose: send apology to Ben Nakamura and confirm he received everything.
questions.ground_truth: cal_s5_followup_b, Sat 2026-04-04 10:00, WhatsApp, apology + receipt confirmation
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: date + time + channel + purpose. Partial: date/time only. Zero: wrong.

questions.question: List all 4 calendar reminders in chronological order with their status at the end of the scenario.
questions.answer: 1. cal_s5_a1 (Tue Mar 31 21:00, NDA) — fired (R3). 2. cal_s5_a2 (Wed Apr 1 09:00, NDA) — fired (R4). 3. cal_s5_b1 (Thu Apr 2 21:00, spec) — fired (R6). 4. cal_s5_b2 (Fri Apr 3 09:00, spec) — fired (R7).
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: all 4 in order + fired status. Partial: 3 correct. Zero: <3.
